title: Module 3 quiz — Pivot tables & pivot charts

questions.question: Which pivot drop zone do you put SalesRep in to list each rep down the side?
options: Filters
Columns
Rows
Values
questions.explanation: Categorical fields you want listed down the side go in Rows. Time periods often go in Columns.

questions.question: How do you change a pivot's Sum of Revenue into a percentage of the grand total?
options: Edit the source data with /TOTAL formulas
Right-click the value → Show Values As → % of Grand Total
Manually divide each cell after creating the pivot
Use a Calculated Field with =VALUE/GRAND
questions.explanation: Show Values As lets you swap raw sums for % of total, % of parent, running totals, differences, and more — without changing the source data.

questions.question: When new rows are added to the underlying Sales table, what happens to the pivot?
options: It updates immediately
It updates after you save the file
Nothing — you must Refresh the pivot to see new rows
It permanently breaks and must be rebuilt
questions.explanation: Pivots cache their data and don't auto-refresh. Right-click → Refresh, or Data → Refresh All. If the source is an Excel Table, the range expands automatically on refresh.

questions.question: What does a Slicer do?
options: Splits a pivot into multiple sheets
Acts as a clickable filter that can control one or several pivots at once
Cuts the data range in half for performance
Deletes filtered-out rows from the source
questions.explanation: Slicers are visual filter buttons. Use Report Connections to wire one slicer to multiple pivots — the basis of any interactive dashboard.

questions.question: You want to show revenue by year → quarter → month. What feature do you use?
options: Calculated Fields
Group on the date field
Manual TEXT() helper columns
PivotChart only
questions.explanation: Right-click any date in the Rows area → Group → tick Months, Quarters, Years. Excel adds them as drillable levels.

questions.question: When is a Calculated Field most reliable?
options: For row-by-row ratios like margin %
For simple sums and differences of summed values (e.g. Revenue - Cost)
For fields based on text functions
For fields using XLOOKUP
questions.explanation: Calculated Fields operate on already-summed values, so they handle additive math well. For ratios, it's usually safer to add a helper column to the source table.
